title: Maximum Number of Robots Within Budget
titleCn: 预算内的最多机器人数目

testcases.input: chargeTimes = [3,6,1,3,4], runningCosts = [2,1,3,4,5], budget = 25 Output: 3 Explanation: It is possible to run all individual and consecutive pairs of robots within budget. To obtain answer 3, consider the first 3 robots. The total cost will be max(3,6,1) + 3 * sum(2,1,3) = 6 + 3 * 6 = 24 which is less than 25. It can be shown that it is not possible to run more than 3 consecutive robots within budget, so we return 3.
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: It is possible to run all individual and consecutive pairs of robots within budget. To obtain answer 3, consider the first 3 robots. The total cost will be max(3,6,1) + 3 * sum(2,1,3) = 6 + 3 * 6 = 24 which is less than 25. It can be shown that it is not possible to run more than 3 consecutive robots within budget, so we return 3.

testcases.input: chargeTimes = [11,12,19], runningCosts = [10,8,7], budget = 19 Output: 0 Explanation: No robot can be run that does not exceed the budget, so we return 0.
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: No robot can be run that does not exceed the budget, so we return 0.
